title: Potential memory leak in AiocoapResourceSubscription on timeout
description:
File: python/src/lichen/client/ip_coap.py, lines 176-187

When the Observe subscription times out or fails, resources may not be fully cleaned up:

```python
async def _results(self) -> AsyncIterator[CoapResult]:
    try:
        first = await asyncio.wait_for(self._handle.response, timeout=self._timeout_s)
        yield first
        iterator = self._handle.observation.__aiter__()
        while not self._closed:
            response = await self._next_observation_or_close(iterator)
            if response is None:
                return
            yield _coap_result(response)
    except Exception as exc:
        raise CoapTransportError(f'{self._method} {self._path} observe failed: {exc}') from exc
        # Note: close() is never called on exception path
```

Issue: When an exception is raised (timeout, network error, etc.), the method raises without calling close(). This means:
1. self._handle.observation.cancel() is never called
2. The aiocoap observation relationship is not properly terminated
3. The server may continue sending notifications

Impact: Resource leak in both client (pending observation handle) and potentially server (continued multicast/observe traffic).

Fix: Use try/finally or contextlib.aclosing to ensure cleanup:
```python
async def _results(self) -> AsyncIterator[CoapResult]:
    try:
        # ... existing code ...
    except Exception as exc:
        await self.close()  # Clean up before re-raising
        raise CoapTransportError(...) from exc
```
